metadata._llmHint: This file contains epic metadata and lightweight storyRefs. Full story content is at: stories/{storyId}.json (relative to this file). Do NOT add full story objects to this file — they belong in standalone story files.

content.title: Integration Platform
content.goal: Connect TaskFlow Pro with the tools teams already use — GitHub for code, Slack for communication — creating a seamless workflow without tab-switching.
content.valueDelivered: Engineers manage tasks without leaving their IDE or chat. PRs auto-link to tasks. Slack commands create and update tasks. Focus mode syncs with Slack DND.

upstream.reason: Integrations need task CRUD API and webhook infrastructure
Slack DND sync needs Focus Mode state

breakdown.description: GitHub App setup, webhook handler, PR-task linking
Slash commands, DND sync, message formatting
Commander.js setup, Ink UI, npm package

implementationNotes: GitHub integration uses GitHub Apps (not OAuth) for fine-grained repository permissions
Slack integration uses Bolt SDK with Socket Mode for development and HTTP for production
CLI tool published to npm as @taskflow/cli with auto-update via npm version checks
All integrations use the same internal task API — no direct database access
content.acceptanceSummary: Epic is complete when GitHub webhook processes PR events correctly, Slack slash commands work in all channel types, CLI is installable via npm with < 5 second startup, and integration tests cover happy path and error cases.

functional.criterion: GitHub webhook processes PR events within 5 seconds
Slack slash commands respond within 3 seconds

nonFunctional.criterion: Integration webhook delivery succeeds > 99.5% of the time

security.criterion: GitHub webhook payloads are verified via HMAC signature
Slack commands only accessible to authenticated TaskFlow users

customerImpact.measurement: Before/after comparison of manual status changes
customerImpact.baseline: 15 manual updates per developer per week

business.target: 60% of teams connect GitHub within 1 month

risks.risk: GitHub API rate limits during large monorepo pushes
risks.impactDescription: Large monorepos with many PRs per day could exhaust GitHub API rate limits, delaying task-PR linking
risks.mitigation: Webhook-based approach (no polling), request queuing with exponential backoff

mitigationStrategies.strategy: Use webhook events instead of polling — eliminates most API calls
Queue webhook processing with rate-aware backoff

risks.contingencyPlan: Implement a manual 'Sync Now' button for users to trigger re-sync if automatic linking is delayed

risks.risk: Slack App approval process may delay launch
risks.impactDescription: Slack's App Directory review process can take 2-4 weeks, potentially delaying the integration launch
risks.mitigation: Submit for review 4 weeks before planned launch date
risks.contingencyPlan: Launch with direct install link (bypassing App Directory) for initial customers
triggers: Slack review takes longer than 3 weeks

items.item: GitHub webhook processes PR events correctly
Slack slash commands work in all channel types
CLI tool installable via npm with < 5 second startup
Integration tests cover happy path and error cases

criteria: Slack commands tested in DM, channel, and thread
CLI tested on macOS, Linux, and Windows

technicalSummary.architecturePattern: Event-driven webhook processing with adapter pattern for each integration

components.name: GitHub Adapter
components.responsibility: Webhook ingestion, PR-task linking, status sync
components.changes: New module with GitHub App authentication

components.name: Slack Adapter
components.responsibility: Slash commands, DND sync, message formatting
components.changes: New module using Slack Bolt SDK

components.name: CLI Tool
components.changes: Standalone npm package with Commander.js and Ink

filesChanged.description: GitHub App webhook handler and PR-task linker
Slack Bolt app with slash commands and DND sync
CLI entry point with Commander.js subcommands
